title: High Point vs Arkansas — Round of 32 Viewing Guide
eventTag: NCAA Tournament • Round of 32 • Portland, OR
venue: Moda Center, Portland, OR • 6:30 PM PT • TNT

stakes.callout: The Cinderella story of the tournament meets the SEC's most explosive offense. High Point — from the Furniture Capital of the World — just won its first-ever NCAA Tournament game by upsetting Wisconsin 83-82 on a go-ahead layup with 11.7 seconds left. It was the scorer's first two-point basket of the entire season. Arkansas destroyed Hawaii 97-78 behind Darius Acuff's 24 points and Calipari's rebuilt juggernaut. The 13-seed Cinderella faces the SEC Tournament champions. Can magic strike twice?

cards.label: For High Point
cards.value: Just won the first NCAA Tournament game in program history — and their first-ever win over a power conference team (previously 0-57). Rob Martin had 23 and 10 assists. Chase Johnston's go-ahead layup was his first two-point basket all season. Now they face an SEC champion. A Sweet 16 from the Furniture Capital of the World would be one of the greatest Cinderella stories ever.

cards.label: For Arkansas
cards.value: Crushed Hawaii 97-78 — Acuff 24/7 assists, Thomas 21, Brazile 19, Ewin 16/12 double-double. Calipari's rebuilt program won the SEC Tournament and is rolling. But Cal has been upset by mid-majors before: Oakland (2024), Saint Peter's (2022). A loss to a 13-seed would revive every "Calipari can't close" narrative.

cards.label: The Style Clash
cards.value: High Point scores 90 PPG (4th nationally) and leads the nation in steals (11.1/game). Arkansas scores 87+ PPG with KenPom's #6 offense. This could be the highest-scoring game of the entire tournament. Both teams want to run — but only one has SEC-level talent.

cards.label: The Calipari Factor
cards.value: John Calipari has 903+ wins and a national championship. He also lost to 15-seed Saint Peter's in 2022 and 14-seed Oakland in 2024. His teams are built to overwhelm with talent, but mid-major chaos is kryptonite. High Point's turnover-forcing defense is exactly the kind of style that has burned him.

styles.title: Chaos, Steals, and the Nation's Best Turnover Margin
bullets: Turnover machines: High Point leads the nation in steals (11.1/game), ranks 2nd in fast-break points (19.16/game), and 3rd in turnovers forced (16.77/game). Their +7.5 turnover margin leads all of D-I. Against Wisconsin, they forced 14 turnovers and scored 19 fast-break points.

First-round upset: Beat Wisconsin 83-82 — trailing by 10 in the first half and by 8 with 7 minutes left. Hit 15 three-pointers. Johnston's go-ahead fast-break layup with 11.7 seconds left — his first two-pointer all season — was one of the wildest plays in tournament history.
Scoring power: 90.0 PPG (4th nationally). 15-0 when scoring 90+. Martin (23 pts, 10 ast vs Wisconsin) and Anderson (16.0 PPG) anchor the offense. Six players can get to double figures.
Ball security: Despite the chaos, High Point ranks 15th nationally in turnover percentage (11.2%). They force turnovers without committing them — a rare and lethal combination.
Size problem: No starter above 6'8". Arkansas has Brazile (6-10), Ewin (6-10), and athletic wings. The rebounding mismatch could negate High Point's tempo advantage.

styles.title: SEC Champions with a Freshman Superstar
bullets: KenPom #6 offense: 50.0% FG, 38.9% from three, and the SEC Player/Freshman of the Year running the show. Acuff (22.9 PPG, 6.5 APG, .445 3PT) is a projected high lottery pick playing the best basketball of his life.

First-round demolition: Beat Hawaii 97-78 with an 11-0 opening run and a 54-36 halftime lead. Outscored Hawaii 64-40 in the paint. Had 26 assists and 11 dunks. Four players scored 16+. This was a statement.

SEC Tournament momentum: Won three games in three days to claim the SEC Tournament — beating Oklahoma, Ole Miss (OT), and Vanderbilt. Acuff averaged 30.3 PPG as Tournament MVP. Brazile hit clutch back-to-back threes to seal the championship.
Five-star freshmen duo: Acuff (22.9 PPG) and Thomas (15.4 PPG) are both McDonald's All-Americans. Calipari called them players with "otherworldly confidence." Thomas is also a lockdown defender.

Calipari's mid-major problem: Lost to 15-seed Saint Peter's (2022) and 14-seed Oakland (2024). High Point's turnover-forcing style is exactly the kind of chaos that has burned him. Defense is KenPom 52nd — if High Point creates turnovers and transition threes, this gets interesting.

keys.title: High Point forced 14 turnovers against Wisconsin. Arkansas's guards are better.
keys.description: High Point's identity is turnover creation — they lead the nation in steals and forced Wisconsin into 14 turnovers. But Acuff (6.5 APG) and Thomas are more skilled ball-handlers than anyone Wisconsin had. If Arkansas can limit turnovers to under 10, their talent takes over. If High Point forces 16+ and converts them into fast-break points, the 13-seed is right back in upset territory — just like against Wisconsin.

keys.title: Can High Point shoot 15 threes again?
keys.description: High Point hit 15 three-pointers against Wisconsin — an absurd number that fueled the upset. Martin, Johnston, and Washington are dangerous from deep. Arkansas's perimeter defense (KenPom 52nd) is worse than Wisconsin's. If High Point gets open looks and the shots keep falling at that rate, they can outscore anyone. If the threes cool off, they don't have the size or interior game to compete with Arkansas inside.

keys.title: The size mismatch inside.
keys.description: High Point's tallest starter is 6'8". Arkansas has Brazile (6-10, 19 points vs Hawaii), Ewin (6-10, 16/12 double-double), and athletic wings who play above the rim. Arkansas outscored Hawaii 64-40 in the paint and had 11 dunks. If this game is decided in the paint and on the boards, Arkansas wins easily. High Point needs to keep the game on the perimeter.

keys.title: Calipari has lost to 15-seeds and 14-seeds. Is a 13-seed next?
keys.description: Saint Peter's (2022) and Oakland (2024) — Calipari's teams have been upset by mid-majors in March. Both times, the chaos and tempo of the underdog disrupted his talented roster. High Point creates more chaos than either of those teams — national leaders in steals, fast-break points, and turnover margin. Calipari knows it. The question is whether his freshmen stars, playing in their first Round of 32, can handle the pressure that comes with being the "expected" winner.

keys.title: Johnston's first two-pointer. What other magic does High Point have?
keys.description: Chase Johnston — a three-point specialist who hadn't made a two-point basket all season — hit a go-ahead fast-break layup with 11.7 seconds left to beat Wisconsin. Owen Aquino then blocked the potential game-winner. These are the kind of improbable moments that define Cinderella runs. High Point plays loose, with nothing to lose, and they've already proven they can deliver in the biggest moments. Arkansas has to respect that.

prediction.summary: High Point has the formula to make this interesting — they lead the nation in steals and forced turnovers, and Calipari has a documented weakness against mid-major chaos (Saint Peter's 2022, Oakland 2024). Rob Martin's 23-and-10 performance against Wisconsin proves the Panthers can perform on this stage. But the size mismatch is brutal. High Point's tallest starter is 6'8". Arkansas has Brazile (6-10), Ewin (6-10), and athletic wings who outscored Hawaii 64-40 in the paint with 11 dunks. If High Point can't replicate the 15 threes they hit against Wisconsin, they don't have the interior game to compete. The Cinderella needs another shooting performance for the ages — and Acuff, the best freshman guard Calipari says he's ever coached, is the kind of talent that makes that very difficult to pull off.

phases.title: Opening 10 Minutes
phases.description: High Point comes out fearless. Martin pushes pace, and the Panthers' ball pressure creates two early Arkansas turnovers — fast-break threes from Johnston and Anderson. Acuff settles things down with 6 quick points, but High Point's energy is undeniable. Panthers trail just 18-16. The Cinderella crowd is buzzing.

phases.title: Mid-First Half to Halftime
phases.description: Arkansas's size takes over. Brazile and Ewin dominate the paint — 3 dunks in a 4-minute stretch. Aquino picks up his second foul trying to defend Ewin, limiting High Point's rim protection and best three-point shooter. Thomas locks down Martin on the perimeter. Arkansas goes on a 16-4 run. Halftime: Arkansas 42, High Point 30. The talent gap is showing.

phases.title: Second Half — One Last Push
phases.description: High Point isn't done. Martin orchestrates a 10-2 run — finding Anderson for a putback and hitting a pull-up three. Johnston drains a deep three and the deficit is 6. Arkansas fans get nervous. Calipari calls a timeout and reminds his freshmen about Saint Peter's. Acuff responds with back-to-back buckets, including a step-back three that silences the Cinderella crowd.

phases.title: Final 8 Minutes
phases.description: Acuff puts the game away. He scores 12 in the final 8 minutes — driving past tired High Point defenders and finishing through contact. Brazile adds a put-back dunk. High Point keeps shooting threes but goes 2-of-8 down the stretch as the legs from two intense games catch up. The Cinderella story ends, but the Furniture Capital of the World has nothing to hang its head about.

projectedScore.rationale: Arkansas's interior dominance and Acuff's brilliance prove too much for the undersized Panthers, though High Point's three-point shooting and turnover creation keep it entertaining longer than the final score suggests.

players.name: Rob Martin
players.stats: 15.3 PPG • 3.7 APG • 23 pts/10 ast vs Wisconsin
players.meta: G • Big South Newcomer of the Year • Riley Wallace Award Finalist
players.bio: The transfer from Southeast Missouri State who transformed High Point. Had 23 points and 10 assists in the Wisconsin upset — controlling the game from start to finish. Averaged 19.3 PPG in the Big South Tournament. Once had 8 steals in a single game. A finalist for the Riley Wallace Award (most impactful transfer in the nation). The engine of the Cinderella run.

players.name: Terry Anderson
players.meta: F • 6-6 • All-Big South • Big South Tournament MVP
players.bio: Transferred from Lamar and became High Point's most complete player. Shoots 56.4% from the field and overpowers smaller defenders inside. Had 22 and 12 against SC Upstate. Big South Tournament MVP. Stole the final inbound pass to seal the Wisconsin upset. The toughness this team is built on.

players.name: Owen Aquino
players.meta: F • 6-8 • Madrid, Spain • Team Block Leader
players.bio: A unicorn: a 6-8 forward from Madrid who leads the team in blocks (1.7/game) AND shoots 45.5% from three. Came through Eastern Florida State (NJCAA All-American) and Liberty. Blocked Nick Boyd's potential game-winning shot against Wisconsin — the play that preserved the upset. Rim protection AND elite perimeter shooting from one player is rare at any level.

players.name: Chase Johnston
players.stats: 14 pts vs Wisconsin • First 2-Pointer All Season
players.meta: G • Three-Point Specialist • The Play of the Tournament
players.bio: A three-point specialist who hadn't made a single two-point basket all season. Then, with 11.7 seconds left against Wisconsin, he drove coast-to-coast on a fast break and hit a go-ahead layup to give High Point the lead 83-82. It was his first two-pointer of the year. The play instantly became one of the most iconic moments of the 2026 tournament.

players.name: Darius Acuff Jr.
players.meta: G • 6-3 • Freshman • SEC POY/FOY • AP First-Team All-American
players.bio:
From Detroit's Cass Tech. McDonald's All-American. First Razorback AP First-Team All-American in nearly 50 years. Scored 49 points against #25 Alabama in double OT — the most by a freshman against a top-25 team ever. SEC Tournament MVP (30.3 PPG). Had 24 and 7 assists against Hawaii. Calipari calls him the best freshman guard he's ever coached — better than Derrick Rose and John Wall.
Read more →

players.name: Meleek Thomas
players.meta: G • 6-5 • Freshman • McDonald's All-American • Pittsburgh, PA
players.bio: From Lincoln Park Performing Arts in Pittsburgh. Chose Arkansas over Pitt and UConn. Won two PIAA state championships. Scored 21 points with 8 rebounds against Hawaii. The second half of Arkansas's five-star freshman backcourt — provides scoring AND lockdown perimeter defense. Calipari: "otherworldly confidence."

players.name: Trevon Brazile
players.stats: 13.2 PPG • 7.4 RPG • 1.6 BPG • 19 pts vs Hawaii
players.bio: The veteran having a breakout senior season. Four double-doubles. Hit back-to-back clutch three-pointers to seal the SEC Championship against Vanderbilt. Had 19 points and 3 blocks against Hawaii. Three consecutive 20+ point games against Auburn, Alabama, and Missouri. The 6-10 presence High Point has no answer for.

players.name: Malique Ewin
players.meta: C • 6-10 • Senior • Ole Miss → JUCO → FSU → Arkansas
players.bio: Transfer path: Ole Miss → South Plains CC → Florida State (led ACC in FG%) → Arkansas. Interior anchor. Had a 16-point, 12-rebound double-double against Hawaii. Shoots .591 from the field. His size and physicality will tower over High Point's undersized frontcourt.

funFacts.title: The Furniture Capital of the World (High Point)
funFacts.body: High Point, North Carolina, is literally the Furniture Capital of the World — home to the biannual High Point Market, the largest furnishings trade show in the world. Now it's also home to a basketball Cinderella. Population: ~114,000.

funFacts.title: "Like a Wild Band of Razorback Hogs" (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: In 1909, coach Hugo Bezdek told fans after a 16-0 win over LSU that his team played "like a wild band of Razorback hogs." Students voted to change from Cardinals to Razorbacks. The live mascot "Tusk" is a Russian boar cared for on a farm near Dardanelle.

funFacts.title: First Two-Pointer of the Season (High Point)
funFacts.body: Chase Johnston — a three-point specialist — hadn't made a single two-point basket all season. Then he drove coast-to-coast and hit a go-ahead fast-break layup with 11.7 seconds left to beat Wisconsin. His first two-pointer. The play of the tournament.

funFacts.title: 49 Points as a Freshman (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: Darius Acuff Jr. scored 49 points on 16-of-27 shooting against #25 Alabama in double overtime — the most by a freshman against a top-25 team ever. Calipari says he's better than Derrick Rose and John Wall.

funFacts.title: $2 Billion Transformation (High Point)
funFacts.body: President Nido Qubein — a Lebanese-American businessman — has invested $2+ billion into High Point since 2005. Enrollment up 322%. Endowment from $42M to $240M. The arena is named after him. A school that was barely known a decade ago is now a Cinderella.

funFacts.title: 40 Minutes of Hell (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: In 1994, Nolan Richardson's "40 Minutes of Hell" press beat Duke 76-72 for the national championship. Scotty Thurman's three with 52 seconds left was the winner. It's Arkansas's only title. A statue honoring Richardson is being built outside Bud Walton Arena. More →

funFacts.title: 0-57 Against Power Programs (High Point)
funFacts.body: Before beating Wisconsin, High Point was 0-57 all-time against power conference teams. Now they're 1-57. And they face another one Saturday. The Furniture Capital is 0-0 in the Round of 32.

funFacts.title: "Woo, Pig! Sooie!" (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: Arkansas's "Calling the Hogs" chant dates to the 1920s and is one of the most distinctive traditions in college sports. In 2014, the university trademarked the sound itself (Registration No. 4,558,864). You can't legally hog-call without permission.

funFacts.title: Medal of Honor at 17 (High Point)
funFacts.body: High Point alum Jack Lucas received the Medal of Honor at age 17 — the youngest WWII recipient — at the Battle of Iwo Jima. He threw himself on two grenades to save his squad. He survived. Other alumni: NASCAR's Austin Dillon and country star Donna Fargo.

funFacts.title: The Basketball Palace (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: Bud Walton Arena seats 19,200 (5th-largest on-campus) and is called "The Basketball Palace of Mid-America." Funded by a $15M donation from Walmart co-founder Bud Walton. All-time home record: 443-104 (.810). In its first season, Arkansas won the national title.

funFacts.title: Calipari's Mid-Major Kryptonite (Arkansas)
funFacts.body: Calipari has been upset by 15-seed Saint Peter's (2022) and 14-seed Oakland (2024). Both times, chaotic, turnover-forcing underdogs disrupted his talent. High Point leads the nation in steals and forced turnovers. History is watching.

funFacts.title: Two Spaniards (High Point)
funFacts.body: Owen Aquino (Madrid) and Conrad Martinez (Barcelona's Joventut Badalona youth system) give High Point two Spanish-trained players. Aquino blocked the potential game-winner against Wisconsin. Martinez played at Arizona. The international pipeline reaches even the Big South.

history.callout: The ultimate David vs. Goliath. High Point has existed in basketball's margins — they'd never won a tournament game or beaten a power conference team before Thursday. Arkansas has a national championship, Calipari's 903 wins, and the SEC Tournament title. High Point is 1-57 against power programs. A Sweet 16 would be the biggest Cinderella story since FGCU's Dunk City in 2013.

subsections.title: High Point: Two Years, Two Firsts

timeline.text: Under Alan Huss, High Point made the NCAA Tournament for the first time in program history. Lost to Purdue 75-63 as a 13-seed. The taste of March was transformative.

Under first-year coach Flynn Clayman, High Point upset #4 Wisconsin 83-82. Johnston's first two-pointer of the season. Aquino's block. Anderson's steal. The first win over a power conference team in school history (0-57 previously).
Nolan Richardson's "40 Minutes of Hell" beat Duke 76-72. Scotty Thurman's three sealed it. Arkansas's only national championship.
After 15 years at Kentucky, John Calipari left for Arkansas. Year 1: Sweet 16. Year 2: SEC Tournament champions, 27-8, and a 4-seed.
Arkansas beat Oklahoma, Ole Miss (OT), and Vanderbilt to win the SEC Tournament for the first time since 2000. Acuff averaged 30.3 PPG as MVP. Then crushed Hawaii 97-78.

subsections.title: The Qubein Investment
subsections.content: High Point's basketball rise is inseparable from Nido Qubein's transformation of the university. The Lebanese-American businessman became president in 2005 and has invested $2+ billion into campus: enrollment up 322%, endowment from $42M to $240M, and an arena bearing his name. When Tubby Smith (1998 NCAA champion at Kentucky) coached at High Point from 2018-21, the investment was beginning to pay athletic dividends. Now, under Clayman, the basketball program is catching up to the campus. A Sweet 16 from the Furniture Capital of the World would complete one of the most unlikely rises in college sports.
